purpose: Single-instance hook (called once in App.jsx) that wires Electron IPC push events to AppContext dispatches and merges init.json + progress files into a unified status object via mergeState(). Owns all live data ingest for the dashboard view: subscribes to 'initialization', 'logs', 'agent_progress', 'all_progress', 'init_state', 'dashboards_list', 'dashboards_changed', 'queue_changed', 'tasks_unblocked', 'write_rejected', 'heartbeat'. Maintains per-dashboard caches in refs (initRef, progressRef, dashboardListRef) to avoid stale-closure bugs. Detects new swarms (task name/created changed) and flushes stale progress cache. Implements connection-health monitoring that re-fetches if no events arrive for 60s.
gotchas: MUST be called exactly once at the App level — calling it twice creates duplicate IPC listeners and double-dispatches.
currentDashboardIdRef is intentionally maintained because the IPC listener closure (registered once on mount) would otherwise see a stale state.currentDashboardId from initial render.
The push-listener useEffect has empty deps [] — the listeners are mounted once for the component lifetime; dispatch is captured via closure.
mergeState examines logs.entries for 'Circuit breaker triggered' WARN entries followed by 'Replanning complete' INFO entries — replanning UI banner depends on these exact log message strings.

Progress merge logic guards against three race conditions: (1) bulk all_progress overwriting more-recent agent_progress, (2) re-fetch overwriting cached entries, (3) leftover stale entries from a previous swarm leaking into a new one. The merge keeps existing entries when their status is more advanced (in_progress > pending, completed > everything, failed > pending).
On 'initialization' push, isNewSwarm detection compares prev.task.name AND prev.task.created — if a new swarm reuses the same name/created, the cache flush won't happen.
fetchDashboardData filters out cached progress entries whose task IDs are NOT in the current init's agent list — prevents prior swarm's progress from showing on the new plan.
Connection health uses a 60s STALE_THRESHOLD with 30s polling — agent_progress AND heartbeat events both reset lastEventTime; if neither fires for 60s, fetchDashboardData re-runs.
deriveDashboardStatus is computed for the SIDEBAR DOT ONLY — uses a different status taxonomy ('idle','waiting','in_progress','completed','error') than the main task.overall_status.
dashboards_list AND dashboards_changed use JSON.stringify equality check before dispatching — avoids unnecessary re-renders AND avoids triggering the SET_DASHBOARDS_LIST auto-switch logic.
tasks_unblocked is dispatched only if data.dashboardId === currentDashboardIdRef.current — silently ignored for non-active dashboards (no global tracking).
write_rejected only console.error's — no UI surfacing yet; if this fires it indicates a hook violation by the master agent.
patterns: Cache + ref pattern: progressRef.current[dashboardId] = ... lets stale-closure-safe IPC handlers read latest cache without depending on React state.
mergeState is pure (init+progress+logs in, status object out) — easy to test and reuse outside the hook.
Listener cleanup uses listenersRef.current array of {channel, handle} — handle is the function returned by api.on(), called to unsubscribe.
Health-check via interval polling lastEventTime — preferred over connection events from main process which are not pushed.
Re-merge effect: useEffect with deps [currentInit, currentProgress, currentLogs] dispatches SET_STATUS — keeps currentStatus derived but in-store for cheap component reads.
conventions: Hook named useDashboardData (singular) — only ONE caller, do not duplicate.
Push event handlers always check data.dashboardId before dispatching to avoid undefined-keyed updates.
